name: server
context: Build MCP servers that expose tools, resources, and prompts to AI applications via the Model Context Protocol
info: This skill covers building MCP servers — the programs that expose capabilities to AI applications. MCP servers can provide tools (executable functions), resources (contextual data), and prompts (interaction templates). The Python SDK provides FastMCP for decorator-based server creation. The TypeScript SDK provides McpServer with Zod schema validation. Servers run on stdio (local subprocess) or Streamable HTTP (remote). Use this skill to create new MCP servers, add tools/resources/prompts, configure transports, and deploy servers for use with Claude Desktop, VS Code, Cursor, and other MCP hosts.
triggers: configure mcp server for claude desktop

sections.title: Python server with FastMCP
sections.body: Install with `pip install "mcp[cli]"`. Create a server with `mcp = FastMCP("name")`. Use `@mcp.tool()`, `@mcp.resource(uri)`, and `@mcp.prompt()` decorators. FastMCP generates JSON Schema from type hints. Run with `mcp.run()` for stdio or `mcp dev server.py` for the inspector. Install into Claude Desktop with `mcp install server.py`.

sections.title: TypeScript server with McpServer
sections.body: Install with `npm install @modelcontextprotocol/sdk zod`. Create a server with `new McpServer({name, version})`. Use `server.tool()`, `server.resource()`, `server.prompt()` methods with Zod schemas for validation. Connect with `StdioServerTransport` for local or HTTP transport for remote.

sections.title: Deployment and configuration
sections.body: Local: configure in `claude_desktop_config.json` with command + args. Remote: set the `url` field pointing to the Streamable HTTP endpoint. Pass environment variables via the `env` field. Use absolute paths for local scripts. Restart the host application after config changes.

sections.title: Logging in MCP servers
sections.body: stdio servers must never write to stdout — it corrupts JSON-RPC messages. Use stderr, a logging framework, or the MCP logging primitive. HTTP servers can log to stdout safely. Use `mcp dev` during development to see server output in the inspector.
